meaning: n.方阵,密集队,密集人群,[解]指骨,趾骨
examples: You would have made your phalanx as deep as you could once you had the width established.
Given such complexity, sorting out who owns what requires time and a phalanx of lawyers.
If whoever brought the turtle to the Seven Site didn't bring the legs, how did a phalanx get there?
It was the front phalanx in a culture war against the "Philistines" to save artistic integrity.
He was surrounded by guys in Mets and Yankees caps in a phalanx of guardian angels.
